page: Amazon Web Services inf1.24xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It contains no local storage and has a GPU count of zero.

In benchmark evaluations, the inf1.24xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores, both placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, with large-block read-only operations reaching 232.48 GB/sec. However, text generation speeds for large language models are limited, with the 135M and 7B models registering in the bottom 10% for text generation, though prompt processing speeds remain average.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, supporting high-throughput network tasks. Given its high compute density, strong multi-core performance, and lack of local storage, the inf1.24xlarge is suited for compute-heavy workloads, static web serving, Redis database operations, and multi-threaded processing tasks that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Amazon Web Services inf1.24xlarge is an x86_64 server built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, zero GPUs, and no local storage. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. Conversely, text generation for large language models performs in the bottom 10%. With its high vCPU count and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is optimized for compute-heavy workloads, high-throughput static web serving, and Redis database operations that do not require local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf1.24xlarge is a Nitro-based server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core CPU performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf1.24xlarge features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute instance featuring ninety-six virtual CPUs, fast memory bandwidth, and high-speed networking for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor
192 GB of DDR4 memory with a 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No local storage or dedicated GPUs included in the configuration
Best suited for compute-intensive tasks, Redis operations, and static web serving
